records.technique_summary: Official-control sampling and analysis governance for mercury levels in foodstuffs.
records.lod_summary: Import rows should preserve lod when available for method-review traceability.
records.loq_summary: Import rows should preserve loq where reported to support lower-bound and review context.
records.recovery_summary: Recovery information should be retained where available to support analytical review context.
records.measurement_uncertainty_summary: Measurement uncertainty should be retained where available for official-control review context.
records.storage_stability_summary: Storage-stability evidence is external to this pack and should be linked separately when available.
records.sampling_plan_summary: Sampling-plan context follows the governed EU official-control regulation and linked EFSA review material.
notes: Preserves analytical-method context for mercury review and monitoring import checks.
Dietary MCP does not execute this method; it records its governed review context.
Analytical-method evidence is exposed as governed sampling, LOQ, recovery, uncertainty, and official-control context.
These records preserve validation context and do not imply a native laboratory-method execution engine.

Analytical-method-evidence record eu.mercury.analytical_method_evidence.official_control: Supporting sources mix historical anchors (efsa.mercury.food.2012) with newer context records (eu.reg.2023_915, eu.reg.333_2007); confirm the older evidence base is still fit for the intended use.
